title: NOC 94103 – Concrete, clay and stone forming operators | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s massive housing and infrastructure push has made Concrete, Clay, and Stone Forming Operators (NOC 94103) some of the most critical players in the construction supply chain. From pre-cast concrete sections for rapid-build housing to high-performance clay materials for sustainable architecture, these operators are at the heart of Canada's "Build Fast" initiative. With billions in federal funding allocated to provincial infrastructure, international workers with experience in automated casting and forming are seeing unprecedented demand.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group941 – Processing and manufacturing machine operators

duties: Machine Operation: Operating and monitoring machines which extrude, mould, press, or form concrete, clay, or stone into specified shapes.
Preparation: Mixing ingredients (sand, gravel, cement, clay, chemicals) according to strict engineering formulas.
Forming: Setting up and adjusting moulds, vibrators, and hydraulic presses to ensure structural integrity.
Finishing: Smoothing surfaces of freshly poured concrete or finishing stone products using power tools.
2026 Update: Utilizing digital sensors to monitor the curing process and moisture levels in "Green Concrete" applications.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is required.
Experience: Experience as a labourer in the same field is often required.
Training: Several months of on-the-job training are typically provided by the employer.
Physical Fitness: Ability to handle heavy materials and work in industrial environments.
Safety: Mandatory WHMIS 2026 and Fall Protection certification (where applicable).
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) expects over 4,000 new job openings in this category due to expansion and retirements. As Canada pivots toward modular construction, operators who can manage pre-cast and pre-fabricated stone/concrete components will be in the highest demand.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is bolstered by Canada's National Housing Strategy:
Ontario (GTA & Ottawa): High demand. Driven by massive transit expansions and the "More Homes Built Faster" Act.
British Columbia: Very Good prospects. Specifically for pre-cast concrete operators supporting seismic-upgrade projects.
Alberta: Good demand. Growing industrial and residential sectors in Calgary and Edmonton are fueling a need for stone and concrete products.
Quebec: Stable demand. Focused on the production of specialized clay and stone materials for heritage restoration and modern commercial builds.
Why Work in Canada as a Forming Operator?
Infrastructure Boom: Your job security is tied to decades-long government infrastructure and housing commitments.
Advanced Manufacturing: Transition from manual labor to operating computerized casting systems and hydraulic presses.
Path to PR: Despite being TEER 4, this NOC is a "Hard-to-Fill" priority in many provinces, opening doors to accelerated immigration.
Specialized Skills: Opportunity to learn carbon-sequestering concrete technology and high-precision stone-cutting.
pathways: Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A highly successful pathway for NOC 94103 workers with a job offer in the province.
Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): This NOC is frequently listed as an eligible occupation for provincial nomination.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for those already working in the province's thriving construction-supply sector.
New 2026 "Construction Category" Draws: Under the revised Express Entry framework, certain semi-skilled construction-related roles are being invited to support the national housing crisis.
